Title: Importing into a new folder
Summary: The folder picker, including how to create folders without leaving the editor.
Body:
## Where you are is where you save

The folder picker shows a directory tree of your Godot project. The current working directory in the picker is the destination on save. There is no separate "navigate then confirm" step; whatever folder you are looking at is where the file lands.

## Creating folders inline

Two ways to create a folder without leaving the editor:

- The "+ New folder" button in the destination header. Creates inside the current folder, then auto navigates into the new folder so the picker is already pointing at it.
- Right click on any folder entry to get a context menu with "Open" and "Create subfolder in <name>...". Drops a new subfolder inside the right clicked folder without first navigating in.

Names are validated as basenames: alnum plus underscore, dash, dot, space. No slashes, no leading dots.

## Conflicts

If a file with the same name exists at the target path, the server returns 409. The editor pops a confirm asking to overwrite. Edit mode always sends `overwrite: true` since that is the whole point.

## Path safety

Every file touching server endpoint validates the resolved target sits under the Godot project root via a relative path check. Filenames must be basenames; no `..` traversal. Atomic writes (temp file plus rename), same as the `.tres` writer.
